question.text: In a book-built public issue, the cap price of the price band must be at least 105% of the floor price but must NOT exceed which percentage of the floor price?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI ICDR regulations stipulate that in book building, the cap price (upper end of the price band) must be at least 105% of the floor price but NOT more than 120% of the floor price. This ensures a reasonable range for price discovery without excessive volatility. **The Trap:** 110% is a common wrong guess because candidates often confuse the minimum spread (105%) with the maximum. The 125% option is tempting for those who overestimate the allowed range. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) tests awareness of NFO pricing but not price band limits. This is specific to NISM Series IX. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the "5-20 rule" — minimum 5% spread, maximum 20% spread from floor to cap in book building.
